title: 7 Things Aesthetics Clinicians do wrong that cause them to fail!
filename: 7 Things Aesthetics Clinicians do wrong that cause them to fail!.en.vtt
excerpt: A really powerful thoughts experiment that you can do to figure out what you should do to grow your business and your practice in Medical Aesthetics is actually to think about how to do the exact opposite when you know this it becomes really obvious what you need to do to succeed…

segments.text: a really powerful thoughts experiment that you can do to figure out what you should do to grow your business and your practice in Medical Aesthetics is actually to think about how to do the exact opposite when you know this it becomes really obvious what you need to do to succeed so here are seven ways that you can really fail at Medical Aesthetics and then don't forget to stay to the end where I'm going to be sharing an additional four-step formula to Growing your confidence the main thing that holds people back to start with I want to take you back to a moment where I nearly quit Medical Aesthetics now looking back this was actually a crucial moment in my career because just on the other side of overcoming that problem all sorts of great things started to happen what happened was I'd had a series of difficult patients some were saying they'd had complications and had a colleague who was really unsupportive who was actually quite happy to see me struggling at this moment I really asked myself the question of whether I was cut out for this career you know you can often feel very much alone and stuck with a difficult patient without any support and this can make you question

whether you're in the right place or not the truth is as I overcame those obstacles all sorts of other things came into play that made the rest of my career much easier of course you're always hitting your head on the next limitation but that was a crucial moment for me so if you've ever found yourself at a low point it's often good to remember that this is often when people bounce and you end up getting to new highs once you've overcome the problems that got you to that position so let's dive in with number one this is what I hear when I'm coaching various delegates the hidden elephant in the room that they're not actually yet moving which is that they plan to start tomorrow there is no better time than today taken action today to move your dreams forward the people who start with tomorrow often leave it for years so start today don't make the mistake of starting tomorrow here's another on a similar vein which is I'm waiting until conditions are perfect this is particularly true with content creators who want a full Production Studio before they pick up

their phone and make a video the condition's a perfect problem is an issue because no matter what happens there's always a reason why you maybe didn't sleep too well or you haven't eaten or you haven't done your hair or you haven't done your makeup right and there's always a reason not to do the thing that you need to do to move forward with your future so don't become one of the people who wait until conditions are perfect do it imperfectly and then get better at it over time the next mistake is made by virtually every clinician when they first start and I have to admit that I also made this mistake and this is when you set your prices all you do is copy the local competition and hope that they're doing it right so this means the most important part of your strategy you've copied from someone quite average so instead of copying a bunch of struggling local businesses instead work backwards from a goal it really matters to you think about the profit margins that would make your struggle worthwhile and then work backwards from that to think about how you become valuable enough to justify those prices this is a far more

strategic way of Designing a business and if you don't do that you end up in the midst of the pile where it's most competitive there are most number of people doing exactly the same thing and it's very hard work so think differently start with the end in mind and don't copy your local competition the next mistake I know that many clinicians make is essentially that they suppress their excitement and optimism for the future so that they better fit in with those around them who are essentially predicting you a more mediocre future this is the problem of getting excited and someone close to you saying sounds like a pipe dream even if they don't say it with words they're saying in their body language they don't really believe that you're going to start your own practice and be successful because they can't imagine doing that for themselves and this is a really common way that people actually hold themselves back is they try and think reasonably like let's not be too ambitious let's not be too excitable let's just pick something that no one around me will be too shocked by and essentially what you're doing there is choosing a very mediocre strategy a

mediocre dream they're actually sometimes the hardest to execute because the motivation is quite low when you have a mediocre dream and the problem with this is that you end up working the same amount of hours for very little extra struggling quite a lot because you're too worried about asking for what you really want you really have to be bold when you set out your first intention and not worry about those around you who may think it's impossible State what you want in life and then design a strategy that you think might get you there the next really big mistake clinicians make is that they start out commoditizing themselves and this is when you think of yourself as just another injector essentially you're weighing yourself up compared with your competition according to the price of a mill of product or a unit that you might be injecting instead of asking yourself what do patients value that I've got because many of you have got tons of people's skills and the ability to connect and Coach people in consultations that you don't price in to your prices patients are absolutely willing to pay for it they are

willing to pay for clinicians who give good explanations who make them feel safe who listen to their problems who really care about the outcome but so often this is not built into the price and you copy someone local to you who's basically a vending machine who doesn't do any of these things that you care about and you then wonder why it's unrewarding dealing with the patients who effectively don't value what you have to bring so find out what's valuable about yourself pitch yourself as valuable in the marketplace to the people who care about that stuff and make sure your prices fit with that particular set of values otherwise you're underselling yourself you're attracting the wrong patients and it's a bit of a struggle to actually get your business going the next one's really a bit controversial but many clinicians wait until they feel confident and that's partly because you like to think of yourself as a safe clinician you shouldn't do things that you aren't confident to do but here's the truth none of us are confident when you start that's not how confidence works it doesn't matter what task you're talking about be a procedure or a video

to social media we all feel unconfident at the beginning and that's because we don't yet fully understand how confidence works it's actually the payment that you get for successfully performing something it's not something that you have before you start the next mistake is actually the first mistake that we all make and I have made this mistake myself and it's essentially to set goals that are actually what you think you can attain rather than what you actually want it's way better to set the goal of the thing you actually want rather than the thing you can actually get although there's more struggle in it you will find a better strategy at the other end of it now there's a poem that really captures this form me and I heard it from a psychologist who I came across called Dr Benjamin Hardy and it's a very useful idea which when I've shared it with many people there is often a little aha moment so let me read this poem to you and see if it makes you think differently about what your goals are I bargained with life for a penny and life would pay no more however I begged at

evening when I counted my scanty store life is a just employer gives you what you ask but once you've set the wages why you must bear the task I worked for amino's hire only to learn dismayed that any wage I'd asked for life would have willingly paid let me give you an example of where I made this crucial mistake when I first started in Medical Aesthetics I took over from my friend Beatrice Dr Beatrice Lai in Hong Kong now she emigrated but she got me into Medical Aesthetics and what I did was I copied her business she worked in a local beauty salon and I thought well that's what you have to do to get started Medical Aesthetics and what I did is I started to scale up that model eventually I we had over 100 different salons around the Manchester area and I was working incredibly hard traveling all over the place and essentially what I'd done is built a business that I hated and I loved all the patients I will say that but I didn't like all the travel and the time it took me and I had

various ideas about how I would get out of that situation but looking back it was a pretty low level goal to have and what I should have done is actually ask myself what do I want my life to be like in five years time and come up with a strategy specifically to achieve that instead of a strategy to scale up someone else's goal which is what we were effectively doing if you ask yourself the question of what you really want and it is an act of Bravery to State this is what that poem effectively says to us you must be brave to State what you really want The Bravery comes because you may not achieve it but I tell you what you're way more likely to achieve it if you state what you really want than if you pretend that you'll just have what everyone else or what you seem to be able to get fairly easily so think more carefully set bigger goals and go after what really matters to you instead of just doing predictable stuff so let me now share with you my final tip this is such a useful idea because so many injectors on my Instagram especially will say how do you get more confident confidence is a big goal that people have and this four-step formula comes

from a business coach who I came across called Dan Sullivan and it's a fantastic idea because he describes the process that you need to go through to become confident it starts with really committing to a goal so whatever your goal is there's a very big difference between being 98 committed and 100 committed when you're 100 committed it will change your physiology to a degree when you can then start to move on to the next step now the next step after commitment is courage the and when you have the courage you can start to move forward to do the things you've not done before now there's a crucial Insight here with courage which is courage does not feel nice courage is actually quite an unpleasant feeling so we often say to people who are courageous that you know good on you and you imagine them feeling strong but that's not courage that's confidence what courage is doing things when you don't actually feel like doing it because there's a risk involved but that's an essential part of developing confidence because once you have the courage to start to implement you get the next C which is capability

if you're actually doing new things that you've never done before you will perceive yourself to be developing capability and only once you've developed capability do you actually get the confidence is a byproduct of executing things you've not done before and to do that you need to be brave and to do that you need to be fully committed so I hope this little summary has helped you clarify your goals and move forward in your Aesthetics career I've also included a free download the five steps to aesthetic success which you can get in the link down below and also don't forget to like And subscribe to make sure that you don't miss further content like this foreign [Music] foreign
